steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which data support the conclusion that nitrogen deposition can offset climate-change damage if change is moderate but not if it is extreme. The decisive clue is "more than offset that effect provided that change is moderate rather than extreme." The correct answer must compare nitrogen vs. no-nitrogen growth across moderate and extreme scenarios, using the current-climate baseline as the reference point.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Growth with nitrogen under the current climate exceeded growth with nitrogen under moderate change, but the latter exceeded growth without nitrogen under extreme change.': Compares nitrogen-treated trees across scenarios but never compares nitrogen-treated moderate growth to the baseline (no-nitrogen current), so it cannot show nitrogen "offsets" damage. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Growth without nitrogen under the current climate exceeded growth without nitrogen under moderate change, but the latter exceeded growth with nitrogen under extreme change.': Shows decline without nitrogen across scenarios and compares to nitrogen under extreme, but this does not demonstrate nitrogen outperforming the baseline under moderate conditions. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Growth with nitrogen under moderate change exceeded growth without nitrogen under moderate change, but the latter exceeded growth without nitrogen under extreme change.': Stays within moderate-change comparisons and shows extreme decline without nitrogen, but never compares back to the no-nitrogen current-climate baseline to show offset. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Growth with nitrogen under moderate change exceeded growth without nitrogen under the current climate, but the latter exceeded growth with nitrogen under extreme change.': Nitrogen at moderate (~0.18 cm) beats the no-nitrogen baseline (~0.16 cm), confirming offset; the baseline (0.16 cm) beats nitrogen at extreme (~0.06 cm), confirming nitrogen fails under extreme change. Both legs of the conclusion are supported. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
